D7 Print L4 Analyze Reference

Registration & Trapping

when one plate prints a hair off-register, white slivers open between colours — trapping deliberately overlaps them to hide the gap

Each ink prints on a separate pass, and the plates must register to a fraction of a millimetre. When one drifts, the boundary between two colours either opens a white sliver or darkens into an overlap. Trapping is the prepress fix: one colour is spread slightly under its neighbour so a small misregister never exposes the paper.

Push the cyan plate off-register and watch a white gap crack open along the seam and a black keyline drift off its fill. Switch trapping on — the colours now overlap just enough that the same shift no longer shows white.
